text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 6分钟英语，来自bbclearningenglish.com

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好，这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Pippa. And I'm Beth. In English, what we say and what we mean are sometimes two very different things.
zh: 我是Pippa。我是Beth。在英语中，我们说的话和我们想表达的意思有时候是两回事。

text: We often use exaggeration for emphasis or for humour – something known as hyperbole.
zh: 我们经常用夸张来强调或制造幽默效果，这种手法叫做hyperbole（夸张）。

text: For example, people say, I am so hungry I could eat a horse. But of course, no one really could.
zh: 比如，人们会说，我饿得能吃下一匹马。但当然，没有人真的能做到。

text: Here's presenter Michael Rosen and linguist Derek Bousfield discussing one example of hyperbole on BBC Radio 4's Word of Mouth.
zh: 接下来是主持人Michael Rosen和语言学家Derek Bousfield在BBC Radio 4节目《Word of Mouth》中讨论一个夸张的例子。

text: People do say absolute nightmare. Yeah, or as my daughters would say, I've had a mare today.
zh: 人们确实会说absolute nightmare（简直是噩梦）。是啊，或者像我女儿会说的，我今天过得像噩梦一样。

text: And when they say mare, which is obviously shortened for nightmare, what it basically means is they've had a mild and potentially slightly humorous occurrence at work.
zh: 当他们说mare的时候，这明显是nightmare的缩写，基本意思是他们在工作中遇到了一些轻微的、甚至可能有点好笑的小状况。

text: You might call an unpleasant or difficult experience a nightmare.
zh: 你可能会把一段不愉快或困难的经历称为a nightmare（噩梦）。

text: Derek's daughter used the word mare, short for nightmare, to describe her terrible day at work.
zh: Derek的女儿用mare这个词，也就是nightmare的缩写，来形容她糟糕的工作日。

text: But she was exaggerating. Her day wasn't really terrible, more like strange or annoying.
zh: 但她是在夸张。她那天其实没那么糟，更像是有点奇怪或烦人而已。

text: Can you remember a time when what you said wasn't really what you meant, Beth?
zh: Beth，你还记得有没有哪次你说的话其实不是你真正想表达的意思？

text: Well, sometimes we say, hi, you alright? Which means, hi, are you alright?
zh: 嗯，有时候我们会说，嗨，你还好吗？意思其实是，嗨，你还好吗？

text: Like, are you okay? but actually we don't really want to hear the answer.
zh: 就像是问你好吗，但其实我们并不是真的想听答案。

text: It just means hello, doesn't it? Yeah, I have a friend who actually just says, alright, and they just mean hello.
zh: 这其实就是打个招呼，对吧？没错，我有个朋友其实只会说alright，他们其实就是想说你好。

text: They don't mean, are you okay? They don't want to hear about my day.
zh: 他们不是真的想问你好不好，也不想听我讲我今天过得怎么样。

text: No, if you're having a bad day, they don't want to know. They're just saying hello.
zh: 对，如果你那天过得很糟，他们其实并不想知道。他们只是在打招呼而已。

text: In this episode, we'll be finding out more about how the meaning of words changes in English.
zh: 在这一期节目中，我们将进一步了解英语中词义是如何变化的。

text: As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 像往常一样，我们会学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: and remember you'll find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记得在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上可以找到本期的所有词汇。

text: But first, I have a question for you Beth. The word hyperbole comes from two ancient Greek words, hupa and balo,
zh: 不过首先，我有个问题要问Beth。hyperbole这个词来自两个古希腊词，hupa和balo，

text: but what is their literal meaning? Is it a to overthrow,
zh: 但它们的字面意思是什么呢？是a：推翻，

text: b to overspeak or c to overthink?
zh: b：说过头，还是c：想过头？

text: think? I have no idea, Pippa. I'm going to say to over -speak.
zh: 想？我不知道，Pippa。我猜是说过头。

text: OK, we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme. It's not just hyperbole that changes the meaning of words.
zh: 好，我们会在节目最后揭晓答案。改变词义的可不只是夸张这一种手法。

text: Its opposite, something called understatement, has the same effect too.
zh: 它的反义手法叫做understatement（轻描淡写），也有同样的效果。

text: Understatement involves de -emphasising something.
zh: 轻描淡写指的是弱化某件事情的程度。

text: For example, imagine you were served an awful meal in a restaurant,
zh: 举个例子，假如你在餐厅吃到一顿难吃至极的饭，

text: but instead Instead of saying how bad the food was, you just said it was less than perfect.
zh: 但你没有说食物有多难吃，而只是说它不够完美。

text: Here's linguist Derek Bousfield discussing understatement on BBC Radio 4's Word of Mouth.
zh: 接下来是语言学家Derek Bousfield在BBC Radio 4节目《Word of Mouth》中讨论轻描淡写的话题。

text: We love understatement as a way of kind of signalling that something much more serious has gone on.
zh: 我们很喜欢用轻描淡写来暗示实际上发生了严重得多的事情。

text: It's a kind of performative, sardonic, not taking oneself too seriously identity when you do that.
zh: 这是一种表演式的、带点讽刺意味的、不把自己太当回事的姿态。

text: Because, you know, taking yourself too seriously is a no -no in British culture.
zh: 因为，你知道，在英国文化中，把自己太当回事是不被认可的。

text: Yes. And so linguistically not taking yourself too seriously is really the done thing.
zh: 是的。所以从语言角度来说，不把自己太当回事才是应该做的。

text: Understatement is valued in British culture, unlike taking yourself too seriously.
zh: 在英国文化中，轻描淡写是被推崇的，而把自己太当回事则不然。

text: If you take yourself too seriously, you think you're more important than you really are.
zh: 如果你把自己太当回事，就意味着你觉得自己比实际情况更重要。

text: In Britain, this is a no -no, a way of behaving that is not socially acceptable.
zh: 在英国，这是一个no-no，也就是一种不被社会接受的行为方式。

text: In other words, it's not the done thing, meaning it's not what you're expected to do in a particular social situation.
zh: 换句话说，这不是the done thing，意思是在特定社交场合中，这不是人们期待你做的事情。

text: Another area where the meaning of words can be easily misunderstood is
zh: 词义容易被误解的另一个领域是

text: apologising. Here, Michael Rosen responds to an imaginary situation where his toe gets accidentally stepped on by Derek Bousfield for BBC Radio 4 programme Word of Mouth.
zh: 道歉。接下来，Michael Rosen在BBC Radio 4节目《Word of Mouth》中回应了一个假想情境：他的脚趾被Derek Bousfield不小心踩到了。

text: Imagine I stepped on your toe and I said, oh Michael, I'm really sorry, and you said, it's all good. Oh, right. That probably means it isn't.
zh: 假设我踩到了你的脚趾，说，哦Michael，真的很抱歉，而你说，没事没事。哦，好吧。那大概意味着其实并不是没事。

text: Yes, I'm actually a bit miffed, a little bit annoyed, but then I don't want to have a barney with you.
zh: 是的，我其实有点miffed，就是有点不高兴，但我又不想跟你吵一架（barney）。

text: You're a stranger, so I don't want to get into a whole thing. So I say, yeah, it's all good.
zh: 你是个陌生人，我不想把事情闹大。所以我就说，嗯，没事没事。

text: It's all good, exactly. It's a bit like, I mean, that's a British English one, an equivalent American English one would be no worries. If someone tells you it's all good,
zh: 没事没事，没错。这有点像……这是英式英语的说法，美式英语里对应的说法是no worries。如果有人跟你说it's all good（没事），

text: they could actually be feeling miffed, an adjective meaning slightly annoyed or hurt by someone's behaviour.
zh: 他们其实心里可能是miffed的，这个形容词的意思是因为别人的行为而略感恼怒或受伤。

text: But no matter how annoyed you are, you probably don't want to get into a barney, a loud argument or quarrel.
zh: 但不管你有多恼火，你大概都不想跟人起a barney，也就是一场激烈的争吵。

text: So you say something like, it's all good or no worries and move on.
zh: 所以你就会说类似it's all good或no worries这样的话，然后就翻篇了。

text: As an English learner, unclear word meanings are a challenge.
zh: 作为英语学习者，词义不明确的确是个挑战。

text: A lot depends on the situation and on the relationship between speaker and listener.
zh: 很大程度上，这取决于具体情境，以及说话人和听话人之间的关系。

text: So don't be shy, get talking to others in English.
zh: 所以不要害羞，大胆用英语和别人交流吧。

text: And if you make a mistake, say no worries and carry on.
zh: 如果你说错了，就说no worries，然后继续往下说。

text: OK Pippa, I think it's time to reveal the answer to your question.
zh: 好了Pippa，我想是时候揭晓你那个问题的答案了。

text: Yes, you thought the ancient Greek words hooper and ballo
zh: 是的，你以为古希腊词hupa和balo

text: meant over -speak, but the answer was actually a – to overthrow.
zh: 的意思是说过头，但正确答案其实是a——推翻。

text: Hooper means beyond and ballo means I throw. Oh
zh: hupa的意思是超越，balo的意思是我投掷。

text: well. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with mare, short for nightmare, meaning an unpleasant or difficult experience.
zh: 哦，好吧。好了，我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇，首先是mare，是nightmare的缩写，意思是一段不愉快或困难的经历。

text: If you take yourself too seriously, you consider yourself and your actions more important than they really are.
zh: 如果你把自己太当回事，就是把自己和自己的行为看得比实际更重要。

text: In Britain, this is a no -no, a way of behaving that is not socially acceptable.
zh: 在英国，这是一个no-no，也就是一种不被社会接受的行为方式。

text: The done thing, on the other hand, is what you are expected to do in a particular social situation.
zh: 而the done thing则是指在特定社交场合中人们期待你做的事情。

text: If you're miffed, you're slightly annoyed by someone's behaviour.
zh: 如果你感到miffed，就是因为别人的行为而略感恼怒。

text: And finally, a barney is a loud argument or quarrel. Once again, our six minutes are up,
zh: 最后，a barney指的是一场激烈的争吵。我们的六分钟又到时间了，

text: But if you're keen to practice these new words and phrases, then check out our website, bbclearningenglish .com,
zh: 不过如果你想多练习这些新单词和短语，可以去我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com看看，

text: where you'll find a worksheet and quiz for this episode.
zh: 那里有本期的练习题和小测验。

text: See you again soon, but for now, goodbye! Bye!
zh: 我们下次再见，现在先说，再见！再见！
